query: is hypoalbuminemia an independent prognostic factor in gastric cancer

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Resection for Malignancy (Gastrectomy) -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Unfortunately, the majority of gastric carcinoma cases are diagnosed in advanced stages, often presenting with symptoms such as weight loss, cachexia, anorexia, early satiety, dyspepsia, gastric outlet obstruction, or malnutrition. The diagnostic workup for gastric cancer entails a range of tests aimed at identifying candidates for gastric resection versus other treatment options. This includes routine laboratory assessments such as baseline hemoglobin, platelet count, and a complete metabolic profile. Serum markers such as albumin and prealbumin can be used to assess a patient's nutritional status, aiding in patient evaluation and treatment planning.

retrieved_docs.title: Hypoalbuminemia -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The presence of hypoalbuminemia is used as a prognosticator for morbidity and mortality in hospitalized patients, in particular in the critical care setting. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Hypopharyngeal Cancer -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Routine bloodwork is important to determine the patient's overall health as well as fitness for surgery and systemic therapy. A complete blood count will reveal normocytic or microcytic anemia from occult blood loss or chronic disease or macrocytic anemia secondary to heavy alcohol use. A renal panel may be warranted to assess the potential tolerance of chemotherapy. Liver enzyme evaluation may identify underlying alcoholic cirrhosis or liver metastases, and serum albumin and prealbumin will help to determine the patient's overall nutritional status, which is a significant prognostic factor for healing after surgery and tolerance of chemotherapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Achlorhydria -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Despite having a risk of developing into gastric adenocarcinoma and gastric carcinoid tumor, achlorhydria has a good prognosis.

retrieved_docs.title: Bruton Agammaglobulinemia (Archived) -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Literature suggests an increased risk of lymphoma, adenocarcinoma of gastrointestinal orgin especially stomach and colon.

retrieved_docs.title: Duodenal Perforation -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Significant risk factors that increase the mortality rate are co-morbidities, resection surgery, presence of shock at admission, female, elderly patients, metabolic acidosis, a delayed presentation of more than 24 hours, acute renal failure, hypoalbuminemia, smokers, and being underweight. [65] [66] Studies show the mortality rate is higher in patients older than 65 compared to younger patients. [65] The postoperative mortality rate in patients with PPU is estimated to be 6% to 10%. [67] Delays in treatment greater than 24 hours, age over 60 years old, concomitant diseases, and systolic blood pressure less than 100 mmHg are the main risk factors that increase the mortality rate. [68]

retrieved_docs.title: Megaloblastic Anemia -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The prognosis for megaloblastic anemia is favorable with proper identification of the precise etiology and the institution of appropriate treatment. [35] Hematologic abnormalities recover with adequate supplementation although neurologic manifestations show some delay in improvement. [33] Timely recognition and supplementation improve the prognosis of this disease, which may have little to no morbidity or mortality associated with it. There are some complications of the disease that can lead to poor outcomes in patients, such as gastric malignancy in patients with pernicious anemia as the cause of megaloblastic anemia. These outcomes, however, are associated with pernicious anemia itself, rather than the megaloblastic disease. Gastric malignancy associated with pernicious anemia is discussed below.

retrieved_docs.title: Myelophthisic Anemia -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The prognosis of myelophthisic anemia varies depending on the underlying cause. For instance, in cases of advanced carcinoma, metastases to the bone marrow are a poor prognosis marker. Kwon JI reported that the prognosis of gastric carcinoma is extremely poor when there are metastases to the bone marrow. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Albumin Colloid -- Indications -- Off-Label Uses
retrieved_docs.content: Preoperative nutrition status: Albumin as a marker of preoperative nutrition status and postoperative outcome in older general surgery patients. Serum albumin is the most used preoperative nutrition parameter. Low serum albumin was a significant preoperative predictor of postoperative complications and mortality. Low serum albumin predicted the prolonged length of stay in the hospital. Serum albumin can be a reliable predictor of postoperative outcomes in older general surgery patients. Whether serum albumin represents a good marker of nutrition status is questionable.

retrieved_docs.title: Hypoalbuminemia -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Hypoalbuminemia is a common finding among hospitalized individuals. The presence of a decreased serum albumin is related to  Increased patient morbidity and mortality. Management of the hypoalbuminemia is contingent on the treatment of the underlying disease.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Stasis (Archived) -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Dehydration Malnutrition. Indigestion Hypoglycemia Hyperglycemia Decreased quality of life.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Evaluation -- Staging Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: For comprehensive preoperative staging, chest and abdominal imaging are necessary to exclude metastasis and determine surgical resectability. [4] Early abdominopelvic computed tomography (CT) is performed to rule out gross metastatic disease. However, it has limitations in assessing T and N stages and detecting small peritoneal metastases, with an overall accuracy ranging from 42% to 82%. Biopsy of any suspicious solitary or oligometastatic sites should be performed, and paracentesis is recommended if malignant ascites is suspected. Chest CT is preferred over plain radiographs for better evaluation. In cases where initial staging is negative for metastatic disease, positron emission tomography combined with CT may be used to assess resectability. Serum markers, including carcinoembryonic antigen, cancer antigen 125, carbohydrate antigen 19-9, and cancer antigen 72-4, have limited diagnostic value and may be elevated due to other conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Perforation -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Over the past 3 decades, the prognosis of patients with gastric perforation has significantly improved. But delays in diagnosis and treatment can still lead to death. Factors linked to high mortality include: Presence of comorbid disease Advanced age Malnutrition Presence of complications Type and location of the perforation

retrieved_docs.title: Pernicious Anemia -- Complications -- Management Guidelines
retrieved_docs.content: British Society of Gastroenterology (2019): "We suggest that a baseline endoscopy with biopsies should be considered in individuals aged ≥50 years, with laboratory evidence of pernicious anemia, defined by vitamin B12 deficiency and either positive gastric parietal cell or intrinsic factor antibodies. As gastric adenocarcinoma affects the corpus in pernicious anemia, biopsies should be taken from the greater and lesser curves (evidence level: low quality; grade of recommendation: weak; level of agreement: 93%)." [39]

retrieved_docs.title: Pernicious Anemia (Nursing) -- Outcome Identification
retrieved_docs.content: With treatment, the anemia will resolve, and the prognosis will be excellent. Some neurologic symptoms, especially if present for a long time prior to diagnosis, may not resolve completely. Delayed treatment can lead to permanent neurological deficits. Most patients will experience at least some improvement in neurologic symptoms when treatment is initiated. If left untreated, severe anemia can lead to heart failure. The risk of gastric cancer persists throughout the patient's life, even if treatment is initiated. Medical noncompliance can lead to a recurrence of all symptoms.

retrieved_docs.content: Gastric adenocarcinoma is the fifth most common cancer in the world. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1764
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of stomach, which is classified under the broader Arabic term سرطان المعدة, is clinically associated with قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض (leukopenia). Additionally, the condition عدم تحمل بروتين اللايسين (lysine protein intolerance) has قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض as one of its manifestations. The presence of leukopenia therefore may serve as an important clinical clue linking both stomach cancer and lysine protein intolerance, underscoring the need for careful hematologic evaluation in patients with these disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 1 -- Community 109
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of stomach is linked to its Arabic counterpart سرطان المعدة, indicating they represent the same disease entity. سرطان المعدة is clinically associated with قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض (leukopenia), a reduction in white blood cells that can reflect bone‑marrow suppression or systemic illness. The SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX is connected to multiple findings: الأشعة السينية على الصَدْر (chest X‑ray) abnormalities, قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض, and gastritis and duodenitis. Gastritis and duodenitis, in turn, are clinically associated with تَغَوُّطٌ مُدَمَّى (hemorrhagic infiltration) and فحص فيروس نقص المناعة البشرية إيجابي (a positive HIV test), and they have a child concept الْتِهابُ المَعِدَةِ التَّآكُلِيّ (erosive gastritis). Hemorrhagic infiltration is clinically associated with فقر الدم الناتج عن فقدان الدم (anemia due to blood loss), linking gastrointestinal bleeding to hematologic compromise. Both فقر الدم الحاد الناتج عن فقدان الدم (severe anemia due to blood loss) and فقر الدم الناتج عن فقدان الدم are members of مصطلحات النزف (باستثناء المصطلحات المختبرية) (SMQ), the standardized set of bleeding‑related terms. Additionally, عدم تحمل بروتين اللايسين (lysine protein intolerance) manifests as قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض, and a فحص فيروس نقص المناعة البشرية إيجابي result is also clinically associated with قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض, underscoring the impact of HIV infection on white‑blood‑cell counts. Collectively, these relationships illustrate how gastric pathology, infectious status, nutritional intolerance, and bleeding disorders intersect, producing overlapping clinical signs such as leukopenia, radiographic changes, and anemia, which are important for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 2 -- Community 950
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of stomach is classified under the broader Arabic term سرطان المعدة, indicating that it is a specific form of stomach cancer. Clinically, سرطان المعدة is associated with قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض (leukopenia), suggesting that a reduced white‑blood‑cell count may be observed in patients with this malignancy. The entity labeled SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX is also clinically associated with both القِصَةُ السينية على الصَدْر (chest X‑ray) findings and قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض, implying that the symptom complex may be identified through radiographic evaluation of the chest and is linked to leukopenia. Additionally, عدم تحمل بروتين اللايسين (lysine intolerance) has قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض as a manifestation, indicating that lysine intolerance can present with a decreased white‑blood‑cell count. Together, these relationships highlight leukopenia as a common clinical feature connecting stomach cancer, the SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX, and lysine intolerance, underscoring its importance in diagnostic assessment and patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 1 -- Community 237
retrieved_docs.content: Stomach cancer (سرطان المعدة) is clinically associated with leukopenia, a laboratory finding that also appears in a SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX, in patients intolerant to lysozyme protein, and in individuals with a positive HIV test. Leukopenia therefore links gastric malignancy to infectious and immunologic contexts. Deficiency‑type anemia is connected to acute blood‑loss anemia (فقر الدم الحاد الناتج عن فقدان الدم) and to leukopenia, reflecting overlapping pathophysiology between iron‑deficiency states and white‑cell depletion. Acute blood‑loss anemia is classified within the bleeding terminology set (مصطلحات النزف … SMQ), which also includes gastroduodenal ulcer (except hemorrhage) and highlights severe gastrointestinal bleeding as a cause of this anemia. Gastritis and duodenitis belong to the same SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX and have a child entity, erosive gastritis (الالتهاب المعدة التآكلي). Both inflammatory conditions are associated with a positive HIV test and with bloody stool (تغوط مدمم);

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 2 -- Community 1303
retrieved_docs.content: Stomach cancer (cancer of stomach, سرطان المعدة) is clinically associated with leukopenia (قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض). Leukopenia also appears as a clinical component of a sign‑symptom complex and is listed as a member of the drug‑interaction syndrome accompanied by eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (متلازمة التفاعل الدوائي المترافق مع كثرة اليوزينيات والأعراض الجهازية, SMQ). Additionally, intolerance to the protein lysine (عدم تحمل بروتين اللايسين) can manifest with leukopenia, linking dietary protein sensitivity to this hematologic abnormality. Together, these relationships highlight leukopenia as a common clinical manifestation linking stomach malignancy, certain drug‑reaction syndromes, and metabolic intolerance, underscoring its importance in patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 1048
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of stomach, also referred to by its Arabic name سرطان المعدة, is linked clinically to a reduction in white blood cells (قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض). The SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX, a collection of clinical findings, is associated with abnormalities seen on a chest X‑ray (الأشعة السينية على الصَدْر), with leukopenia, and with the condition gastritis and duodenitis. Lysine protein intolerance (عدم تحمل بروتين اللايسين) manifests as leukopenia, reinforcing the connection between metabolic intolerance and hematologic abnormalities. A positive HIV test (فحص فيروس نقص المناعة البشرية إيجابي) is also clinically associated with leukopenia and with gastritis and duodenitis, indicating that immunodeficiency may predispose to both blood cell deficits and upper gastrointestinal inflammation. Gastritis and duodenitis is situated within a hierarchical relationship: it is a specific form of gastritis (الْتِهابُ المَعِدَة) and it gives rise to erosive gastritis (الْتِهابُ المَعِدَةِ التَّآكُلِيّ). Together, these interconnections highlight how stomach cancer, immune status, hematologic changes, and gastrointestinal pathology are intertwined, underscoring the importance of integrated assessment in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2741
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is identified as an etiological factor for قَرْهَة مَعِدِيّة and for الالتصاق. This indicates that the same qualifying element is considered a cause or contributing factor for both gastric ulcer and the formation of adhesions. Recognizing a common etiology linking these two conditions can help clinicians consider overlapping pathogenic mechanisms when evaluating patients with gastrointestinal ulceration or postoperative adhesions, potentially guiding more integrated prevention and treatment strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 2 -- Community 630
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of stomach, also referred to by its Arabic term سرطان المعدة, is clinically associated with قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض (low white blood cell count). The presence of قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض can manifest as عدم تحمل بروتين اللايسين (lysine protein intolerance) and is also linked to فحص فيروس نقص المناعة البشرية إيجابي (a positive HIV test). These interconnections suggest that patients with stomach cancer may develop leukopenia, which may present with lysine intolerance and may be observed in the context of HIV infection, underscoring the importance of integrated assessment and management of these related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 1811
retrieved_docs.content: Chemically induced and congenital are recognized qualifiers that can be applied to a range of disorders. Both qualifiers may be used to describe تمزق (tear), اعتلال الكلية السكري (diabetic kidney disease), قَرْحَة مَعِدِيّة (gastric ulcer), فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الحديد (iron‑deficiency anemia), تفاعل نقل الدم (transfusion reaction) and التسمم الوشيقي (septicemia). In addition, each qualifier can qualify انسداد صفراوي خارج الكبد (extra‑hepatic biliary obstruction), خلل الحركة المتأخر (delayed gastric emptying), فرط البروتينات الشحمية (hyperlipoproteinemia), فطار غزوي (peptic ulcer) and عضال غدي (glandular tumor). This indicates that both chemically induced and congenital etiologies are considered relevant descriptors across a broad spectrum of gastrointestinal, hematologic, renal, infectious and neoplastic conditions, which is important for accurate classification, diagnosis and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 1166
retrieved_docs.content: Stomach cancer, referred to as “cancer of stomach” and also named سرطان المعدة in Arabic, is clinically associated with a low white‑blood‑cell count (قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض). This leukopenia is further linked to a broader SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX and is commonly observed in patients who have a positive HIV test (فحص فيروس نقص المناعة البشرية إيجابي). Consequently, leukopenia serves as a shared clinical finding that connects gastrointestinal malignancy, HIV infection, and a constellation of signs and symptoms, underscoring its importance for diagnosis, monitoring, and integrated patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 1054
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of the stomach, also identified by its Arabic name سرطان المعدة, is clinically associated with leukopenia (قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض). In addition, a positive HIV test (فحص فيروس نقص المناعة البشرية إيجابي) is likewise clinically associated with leukopenia. Consequently, leukopenia may be observed as a common hematologic abnormality in patients with stomach cancer as well as in individuals who test positive for HIV, underscoring its significance in the clinical evaluation and management of these conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2861
retrieved_docs.content: The designation “Allowed qualifier” is applicable as a qualifier for both enzymology and etiology across a spectrum of disorders, including gastric ulcer (قَرْحَة مَعِدِيّة), iron‑deficiency anemia (فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الحديد), ureteral obstruction (انسداد الحالب), acute cholestasis of the liver (ضُمورُ الكَبِد الأصفر الحادّ), smooth muscle tumor (ورم عضلي أملس), and other disorders of the stomach and duodenum. Using this allowed qualifier enables precise specification of the enzymatic mechanisms and underlying causes for each condition, thereby enhancing clarity in clinical documentation, research analysis, and patient‑care planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 2 -- Community 664
retrieved_docs.content: Stomach cancer, referred to in Arabic as سرطان المعدة, is a specific type of gastric malignancy that is clinically associated with leukopenia (قِلَّةُ الكُرَيَّاتِ البِيْض). Leukopenia, in turn, is classified as a member of the drug‑interaction syndrome accompanied by eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (متلازمة التفاعل الدوائي المترافق مع كثرة اليوزينيات والأعراض الجهازية (SMQ)). This network of relationships indicates that patients with سرطان المعدة may develop low white‑blood‑cell counts, a hematologic abnormality that falls within a broader syndrome of drug‑related reactions characterized by eosinophil elevation and systemic clinical features. Awareness of this connection is important for clinicians to monitor blood counts, anticipate potential drug interactions, and provide comprehensive care for individuals affected by stomach cancer.
